step_by_step_thinking:
Phenylbutyrate (phenyl acetate) is commonly used to treat hyperammonemia in urea cycle disorders because it functions as an ammonia scavenger medication, helping to remove ammonia from the body through an alternate route.
\boxed{B} Based on the evidence provided about the mechanisms and effectiveness of different therapies for managing hyperammonemia in urea cycle disorders, phenylbutyrate stands out as a recognized method alongside sodium phenylacetate and sodium benzoate to scavenge ammonia effectively. Thus,
